title: Jabir ibn Hayyan
subtitle: The corpus that built Arabic alchemy, 8th–9th c.

narrative.intro: 'Jabir' is as much a library as a man — hundreds of treatises, the sulphur–mercury theory, the science of balances. The dots trace where the corpus was written, read, and disputed.
narrative.provenance_note: Events involving Jabir ibn Hayyan (14 in corpus). The historical Jabir is disputed; the map plots the corpus's footprint, not a biography.
narrative.design_note: Illuminated Manuscript: the Jabirian corpus survives as manuscript culture; gold-on-parchment is its native dress.
